assess.title: Analyze Unit 3 Assessment Results
assess.desc: Unit 3 end-of-unit assessment was given Friday. Analyze results to identify gaps in slope, y = mx + b, and calculating slope from two points before diving into Unit 4.

revise.title: Revise Warm-Ups to Practice Slope
revise.desc: Use coteach.ai to revise Unit 4 Lesson 3-4 warm-ups so students get extra practice on slope calculation — the biggest gap from Unit 3. Also integrate the hanger-to-equation game into Lesson 3.

oko-slope.title: OKO Small Group: Calculating Slope
oko-slope.desc: Jayden, Aisha, and Carlos all struggled with calculating slope from two points on the Unit 3 assessment. Set them up with OKO to practice finding slope and interpreting rate of change in real-world contexts from Lessons 7 and 10.

marcus.title: Extra Practice: Marcus
marcus.desc: Marcus confuses slope and y-intercept, so he needs support from Unit 3 Lessons 5–8. Assign him 1-on-1 practice on writing equations in y = mx + b form from a graph.

aaliyah.title: Extra Practice: Aaliyah
aaliyah.desc: Aaliyah has a gap from Grade 7 Unit 2 on proportional relationships (y = kx) that's blocking her understanding of linear vs. proportional. Assign her 1-on-1 practice identifying the constant of proportionality.
aaliyah.math: y = kx: if 3 pizzas cost $24, find k

oko-equations.title: OKO Group: Writing Equations from Tables
oko-equations.desc: Maya, DeShawn, and Imani can read tables but aren't connecting rate of change to slope yet. Set them up with OKO to practice writing y = mx + b from tables, building on Unit 3 Lessons 5–7.

challenge.title: Challenge: Equation Error Analysis
challenge.desc: Darius and Zara aced the Unit 3 assessment and are ready to move ahead. Give them Unit 4 Lesson 4 cool-down style problems where they critique solution paths and find errors in multi-step equations.

analyze.title: Analyze Practice for Common Bugs
analyze.desc: Review Unit 3 cool-downs and practice problems to find patterns in student errors around slope, y-intercept, and writing equations of lines
analyze.math: Bug: reads y-int as slope in y = 3 + 2x

video.title: Create Video: Slope from Two Points
video.desc: Record explainer video students watch at home on calculating slope from two points, using rise/run and the formula — the most common Unit 3 gap

game.title: Build Hanger Diagram Game
game.desc: Build a contrasting cases game where students see a hanger diagram side-by-side with an equation and decide if they match. Uses research on transfer (Bransford & Schwartz, 1999) to make the hanger→equation connection explicit. Runs in Brisk for live progress tracking.

assign-game.title: Assign Game to Class
assign-game.desc: Push the hanger diagram game to all students as extra practice after Unit 4 Lesson 3 Balanced Moves

timeline.text: ── Monday, Nov 3 ──
Morning! I gave the Unit 3 assessment on Friday. Can you analyze the results while I teach Lesson 1 today? I'm jumping straight into Unit 4.
On it. I'll have the results analyzed within the hour. Here are the first tasks to get started — I'll add more once we see the assessment data.
✓ 4 tasks created
Looks good. Let me approve these.
✓ 4 approved
Starting the assessment analysis and the OKO equations group. Maya, DeShawn, and Imani will work with OKO while you teach Lesson 1.
→ Assessment Analysis & OKO Equations → In Progress
Just finished Lesson 1 — Writing Equivalent Equations. Most kids got the idea that 2x + 3 = 7 and 2x = 4 are equivalent, but about a third couldn't explain WHY. They just want to memorize steps.
That's common with Lesson 1. For tomorrow's Lesson 2 on hanger diagrams, try having students explain the 'why' using the balance metaphor before writing any symbols.
OKO session complete! Maya nailed rate of change from tables (92%). DeShawn (85%) still confuses the initial value with the first y-value. Imani (88%) is solid.
✓ OKO Group: Writing Equations from Tables → Done
── Tuesday, Nov 4 ──
Looking at Unit 3 assessment data:

• 58% struggled to calculate slope from two coordinate points
• 6 students can't reliably write y = mx + b from a graph
• Aaliyah, Jayden, and 3 others still shaky on proportional relationships (Grade 7 gap)
• Darius and Zara aced everything — ready for extension
→ Assessment Analysis → Review
Based on these results, I'm recommending additional tasks. Jayden, Aisha, and Carlos need a slope group on OKO. And I'd suggest revising Lessons 3–4 with coteach.ai once we see how Lesson 2 goes.
✓ 3 new tasks added from assessment data
Good call on the slope group. Approve OKO and the lesson revision. But hold off on the challenge for Darius and Zara — let me see how they do in class first.
Got it — OKO slope and revision approved, challenge on hold. Starting Marcus on Podsie and the slope group on OKO.
→ OKO Slope & Marcus → In Progress

Just taught Lesson 2 — Keeping the Equation Balanced. The hanger diagrams were a hit! But Jayden and a few others kept wanting to 'solve' without explaining the balance. Marcus was really lost — glad he's getting Podsie time.

Good to hear the hangers worked. OKO is specifically working on the 'why' with Jayden. And Marcus just had a breakthrough on Podsie — he was reading the coefficient of x as the y-intercept. After working through three graphs, he got 11/12 correct.
That's great! I noticed the same m/b confusion in class. I'll check in with him tomorrow.
✓ Extra Practice: Marcus → Done
── Wednesday, Nov 5 ──

OKO slope group wrapped up. Aisha and Carlos can now calculate slope from two points. During the OKO session, Carlos started participating much more — he was pushing Jayden and Aisha to prove their slope calculations were right. OKO flagged a 40% increase in his engagement. Jayden still reverses rise and run sometimes — flagging for follow-up.
✓ OKO Slope group → Done
Starting Aaliyah's Podsie practice. Also — assessment analysis approved, unlocking the lesson revision with coteach.ai.
🔗 Dependency resolved: Assessment → Lesson Revision

Just finished Lesson 3 — Balanced Moves. TOUGH. When we moved from hanger diagrams to actual equations, about half the class couldn't connect the moves. The hanger-to-equation transition is the real sticking point.

The research on transfer says you need to make that connection really explicit — for instance with contrasting cases (Bransford & Schwartz, 1999). I could build a game where students see a hanger side-by-side with an equation and decide if they match. We can run it in Brisk so you can see progress live.
Love it — build it. And have coteach.ai fit it into the Lesson 3 warm-up for next time.
✓ 2 new tasks added: Build game & Assign to class
Also — Darius and Zara are clearly ahead. Want to approve that challenge card now?
Yes, they're ready. Approve it.
Aaliyah completed Podsie practice — she can now distinguish y = kx from y = mx + b. That Grade 7 gap is closed. Starting the challenge for Darius and Zara, and building the contrasting cases game.
✓ Aaliyah → Done · Challenge & Game started
── Thursday, Nov 6 ──

coteach.ai finished revising the warm-ups with extra slope practice and the game integration. Hanger game is built and ready. I also want to suggest a practice bug analysis and a slope video for the end of the week.
✓ 2 new tasks added: Bug analysis & Slope video
Yes to all of those. Approve them.
→ Bug analysis → In Progress

Lesson 4 went WAY better! Both the coteach changes to the warm-up AND the contrasting cases game were a hit. DeShawn was explaining to his table partner why a hanger didn't match the equation. Those were game changers.
That's great to hear! The contrasting cases approach really works for making the hanger→equation connection explicit. Practice analysis found the top 3 bugs:

1. Confusing slope and y-intercept when equation is y = b + mx (41%)
2. Calculating slope as run/rise instead of rise/run (28%)
3. Not recognizing that a negative slope means the line goes down (19%)

Creating a video on slope from two points.
That y = b + mx confusion is real — I see it every year. Make sure the video shows both forms side by side.
🔗 Dependency resolved: Analysis → Video creation · Game → Done
── Friday, Nov 7 ──
Last day! Video is rendered with both equation forms side by side. Darius and Zara crushed the error analysis — they found all 4 errors in Lin's solution path.
→ Video → Review · Challenge → Done
The revised warm-ups and game look great — approve them. Push the slope video for weekend homework and assign the hanger game as extra practice.
🔗 Dependency resolved: Game → Assign to class

Week complete! 🎉

📚 Lessons 1–4 taught — the contrasting cases game and the coteach warm-up revisions were game changers
📊 Unit 3 gaps identified — slope from two points was the biggest
👥 Both OKO groups closed key gaps (Carlos had a 40% engagement jump!)
👤 Marcus and Aaliyah caught up via Podsie
🌟 Darius & Zara completed error analysis extension
🎬 Slope video assigned for weekend
🎮 Contrasting cases game assigned for extra practice

Great week. Lessons 1–4 are done and I feel way better about where the kids are. The contrasting cases game and the coteach revisions were game changers — DeShawn explaining why a hanger didn't match the equation was the highlight. See you Monday for Lesson 5! 🙏
✓ All tasks complete · Lessons 1–4 taught · On to Lesson 5 Monday!
